subject: Physics
title: The Quantum Eraser
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Quantum Eraser.

hook.text: Mark which slit a photon goes through and the interference pattern vanishes. Erase the mark and it returns.

buildup.text: In the double-slit experiment, photons create an interference pattern when no one checks which slit they pass through.

discovery.text: Adding a detector to mark which slit destroys the pattern. The photon acts like a particle.

twist.text: But if you erase the which-path information after the photon passes, the interference pattern reappears.

climax.text: The photon's behavior seems to change retroactively based on whether information is kept or erased.

punchline.text: Erase the evidence and waves return. Observation shapes reality.

quiz.question: What happens in the quantum eraser experiment when which-path information is erased?
